speakers.name: Mr. C.S. Setty
direct_answers: We are not waiting because we have done the QIP.
The 12% to 14% guidance is across the segments, not necessarily Home Loans.
dodged_or_vague: I gave a little longer answer so that again this question on ECL does not come.
speakers.summary: Mr. C.S. Setty, the Chairman of SBI, delivered a confident and comprehensive overview of the bank's performance and strategic direction. He emphasized the bank's strong performance in Q2 FY26, highlighting industry-leading credit growth and improved NIMs. Setty discussed the global and domestic economic outlook, projecting cautious optimism for India's growth. He also detailed the bank's successful capital raise of Rs 25,000 crores and outlined strategic anchors such as brand trust and liability franchise strength. Setty's responses to analyst questions were mostly direct, though he occasionally provided extended answers to preempt further queries.

speakers.name: Mr. Pawan Kumar
speakers.role: General Manager (Performance, Planning and Review), State Bank of India
speakers.summary: Mr. Pawan Kumar, as the General Manager of Performance, Planning and Review, opened the call with a neutral tone, welcoming participants and setting the stage for the presentation of the Q2 FY26 results. He provided a standard safe harbour statement, indicating that some statements may be forward-looking and subject to change. His role was primarily to facilitate the proceedings and ensure a smooth transition to the Chairman's remarks.

speakers.name: Mr. Ashok Ajmera
speakers.summary: Mr. Ashok Ajmera, Chairman of Ajcon Global, expressed optimism about SBI's performance and valuation. He congratulated the bank on reaching a business size of 100 trillion and compared its market cap with peers, suggesting that SBI deserves higher valuations. Ajmera highlighted the bank's low GNPA and net NPA ratios and questioned the disparity in price-to-earnings ratios. His remarks were supportive and aimed at reinforcing confidence in SBI's market position and future prospects.

chain_of_thought: The transcript reveals three main speakers: Mr. C.S. Setty, Mr. Pawan Kumar, and Mr. Ashok Ajmera. Mr. Setty, as the Chairman, provided a detailed overview of SBI's performance and strategic direction, maintaining a confident tone throughout. He addressed several analyst questions directly, though he occasionally gave extended answers to preempt further queries. Mr. Pawan Kumar facilitated the call, delivering introductory and procedural remarks with a neutral tone. Mr. Ashok Ajmera, an analyst, expressed optimism about SBI's valuation and performance, comparing it favorably with peers and suggesting it deserves higher market valuations. Each speaker's contribution was distinct, with management focusing on performance and strategy, while the analyst highlighted valuation and market comparisons.
